​​​​​​​
solution:
Arrhenius suggested an equation which describes rate constant,k as a function of temperature, T.

k=Ae-Ea/RT

A=frequency factor

Ea=Activation energy

In the Arrhenius equation the factor e -Ea /RT corresponds to the fraction of molecules that have kinetic energy greater than Ea

lnk=-EaRT+lnA

Y=C+  M  X

On plotting lnk against 1/T we get a straight line.The slope of the graph gives activation energy and intercept gives frequency factor

​​​​​​​

slope=-EaR=-ΔyΔx

Slope =EaR=205

Ea=4R=8Cal/mol

q:
Two statements in respect of drug-enzyme interaction are given below

Statement I : Action of an enzyme can be blocked only when an inhibitor blocks the active site of the enzyme.

Statement II : An inhibitor can form a strong covalent bond with the enzyme.

In the light of the above statements. Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Two statements in respect of drug-enzyme interaction are given below

Statement I : Action of an enzyme can be blocked only when an inhibitor blocks the active site of the enzyme.

Statement II : An inhibitor can form a strong covalent bond with the enzyme.

In the light of the above statements. Choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
The first function of an enzyme is to hold the substrate for a chemical reaction. Active sites of enzymes hold the substrate molecule in a suitable position, so that it can be attacked by the reagent effectively.

Substrates bind to the active site of the enzyme through a variety of interactions such as ionic bonding, hydrogen bonding, van der Waals interaction or dipole-dipole interaction.

Drugs inhibit activities of enzymes. These can block the binding site of the enzyme and prevent the binding of substrate, or can inhibit the catalytic activity of the enzyme. Such drugs are called enzyme inhibitors

Some drugs do not bind to active sites. These bind to different site of enzyme called allosteric sites.

This binding of inhibitor at allosteric site changes the shape of the active site in such a way that substrate cannot recognise it

q: The correct decreasing order for metallic character is
question: The correct decreasing order for metallic character is
solution:
A metal is a material that, when freshly prepared, polished, or fractured, shows a lustrous appearance, and conducts electricity and heat relatively well. Metals are typically malleable (they can be hammered into thin sheets) or ductile (can be drawn into wires)

On moving top to bottom of the periodic table metallic character increases and on moving left to right metallic character decreases.

Metallic character is directly proportional to the atomic size.

The correct order of atomic size of the given elements is Na>Mg>Be>Si>P.

Hence, correct order of metallic character is Na>Mg>Be>Si>P

All these elements are form 3rd period except beryllium which is from second period

Na is an alkali metal

Mg and Be are alkaline earth metals, 

Phosphorous belongs to a group which is commonly called as pnicogens.

Si belongs to 14th group

solution:
Ni is in zero oxidation state in Ni(CO)4 so the electronic configuration of Ni is 3d84s2. As CO is a strong ligand, it pushes all the electrons in the 3d orbital, therefore the hybridisation of Ni(CO)4 is sp3 and it has tetrahedral geometry. It is diamagnetic due to the absence of unpaired electrons.

In [Ni(CN)4]2-, there is Ni2+ ion for which the electronic configuration in the valence shell is 3d84s0.

In presence of strong field CN- ions, all the electrons are paired up.

The empty, 3d, 3s and two 4p Orbitals undergo dsp2 hybridization to make bonds with CN- ligands in square planar geometry.

The atomic number of Co is 27 and its valence shell electronic configuration is 3d74s2.

Co is in +3 oxidation state in the complex [CoF6]3-.

[CoF6]3- is sp3d2 hybridized and it is octahedral in shape.

Another Co3+ complex, [Co(CN)6]3-, is diamagnetic and has no unpaired electrons. The hybrid orbitals used to form this complex are d2sp3.

CoCN63- Hybridisation d2Sp3

The mole fraction of substance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> in the vapour phase and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn></math> in the liquid phase. The vapour pressure of pure liquid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is____atm.(Nearest integer)
solution:
Raoult’s law states that a solvent’s partial vapour pressure in a solution (or mixture) is equal or identical to the vapour pressure of the pure solvent multiplied by its mole fraction in the solution.

Raoult’s law is valid only in the case of ideal solutions

PA=PoAχAAccording to Raoult's law

PA=PTYAAccording to Dalton's lawPT=Total pressurePA=vapour pressure of A in solution

PoA=Vapour pressure of pure liquid A

χA=Mole fraction of component A in liquid phase

YA=Mole fraction of component A in gaseous phase

So, PA=PoAχA=YAPT

PAo0.2=0.5×0.8

PAo=2 atm

q:
Which of the following pair is not isoelectronic species?
(Atomic numbers Sm=62; Er=68; Yb=70; Lu=71; Eu=63; Tb=65; Tm=69)
question:
Which of the following pair is not isoelectronic species?
(Atomic numbers Sm=62; Er=68; Yb=70; Lu=71; Eu=63; Tb=65; Tm=69)
solution:
Isoelectronic species refers to two atoms, ions or molecules that have the same electronic structure and the same number of valence electrons term means "equal electric" or "equal charge". Isoelectronic chemical species typically display similar chemical properties.

Sm2+→electron=60Er3+→electron=65 Tb2+→electron=63Tm4+→electron=65 (not isoelectronic)

While Yb2+ and Lu3+ both will have 68 electrons. and Eu2+ and Tb4+ both will have 61 electrons.so, they are isoelectronic with each other.

Note: According to NTA only option D is given as answer but option A and D both will be the correct answer.

solution:
De Broglie's equation for calculating the wave length(λ) associated with the electron is given below

λ=hp=hmV

Let Kinetic energy of the particle of mass 'm' is K

K=12mV22Km=m2V22Km=mV

λ=h2mK

h=Planck's constant=6.62×10-27erg-sec

K=h22 mλ2

K=h22 mλ2=43.9×10-682×9.1×10-31×10.89×10-20

K=2.215×10-18

Eabs=Ereq+KEabs=Energy absorbedEreq=Energy requiredK=Kinetic energyEabsEreq=1+KEreq=1+2.215×10-1813.6×1.602×10-19=2.0166

q: Among the following the number of curves not in accordance with Freundlich adsorption isotherm is
question: Among the following the number of curves not in accordance with Freundlich adsorption isotherm is
solution:
Freundlich, in 1909, gave an empirical relationship between the quantity of gas adsorbed by unit mass of solid adsorbent and pressure at a particular temperature. The relationship can be expressed by the following equation

Xm=KP1n

Where x is the mass of the gas adsorbed on mass m of the adsorbent at pressure P, k and n are constants which depend on the nature of the adsorbent and the gas at a particular temperature. The relationship is generally represented in the form of a curve where mass of the gas adsorbed per gram of the adsorbent is plotted against pressure.

logxm=1nlogp+logk

Freundlich isotherm explains the behaviour of adsorption in an approximate manner. The factor 1n can have values between 0 and 1

question:
Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R

Assertion A : The reduction of a metal oxide is easier if the metal formed is in liquid state than solid state.

Reason R : The value of ΔGΘ becomes more on negative side as entropy is higher in liquid state than solid state.

In the light of the above statements. Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are correct and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is NOT the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is not correct

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is not correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is correct
solution:
The entropy is higher if the metal is in liquid state than when it is in solid state. The value of entropy change (∆S) of the reduction process is more on positive side when the metal formed is in liquid state and the metal oxide being reduced is in solid state. Thus the value of ∆fG becomes more on negative side and the reduction becomes easier.

ΔG=ΔH-TΔS

∵ Entropy of liquid is more than solid

∴ on melting the entropy increases and ΔG becomes more negative and hence it becomes easier to reduce metal.

question:

Volume V

Heat q

solution:
A property whose value doesn't depend on the path taken to reach that specific value is known to as state functions or point functions. State functions are the values which depend on the state of the substance like temperature, pressure or the amount or type of the substance, as a matter of fact, state functions do not depend on how the state was reached or established.

Among the given functions Internal energy, enthalpy, volume,  are the state functions.

The state of a thermodynamic system is described by its  macroscopic properties.

solution:
Let the coefficients of the middle terms in the expansion of 16+βx4,1-3βx2 and 1-β2x6,β>0, respectively form the first three terms of an A.P. If d is the common difference of this A.P., then 50-2dβ2 is equal to _____ .

Given,

Coefficients of middle terms of given expansions 16+βx4, 1-3βx2 and1-β2x6 are C2416β2, C12-3β & C36-β23 respectively,

Now it is given that middle terms are forming an A.P

So, 2C12-3β = C36-β23+C2416β2

⇒2.2-3β=β2-5β32

⇒-24=2β-5β2

⇒5β2-2β-24=0

⇒5β2-12β+10β-24=0

⇒β5β-12+25β-12=0

⇒β+25β-12=0

So, β=125 and β=-2 {which is not possible as β>0}

Now d=C12-3β-C2416β2=-6β-β2

Now putting the value of β & d in 50-2dβ2 we get,

50-2dβ2=50-2-6β-β2β2=50+12β+2=57

options: real and exactly one of them is positive
solution:
Given α,β be the roots of the equation x2-2x+6=0 

So sum of roots will be α+β=2 and product of roots will be αβ=6

And also given 1α2+1 and 1β2+1 are roots of x2+ax+b=0

So sum of roots will be -a=1α2+1+1β2+1

⇒a=-1α2-1β2-2 .....1

And similarly product of roots will be,

b=1α2+1β2+1+1α2β2 ....2

Now adding equation 1 & 2 we get,

a+b=1αβ2-1=16-1=-56 {as αβ=6}

Now putting the value of a+b in x2-a+b-2x+a+b+2=0

⇒x2--56-2x+2-56=0

⇒6x2+17x+7=0

⇒x=-73,x=-12 are the roots, both roots are real and negative.

question: The distance of centre of mass from end <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> of a one dimensional rod <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> having mass density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ρ</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>ρ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>L</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> and length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> (in meter) is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>L</mi></mrow><mi>α</mi></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is _____ (where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is the distance form end <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>)
solution:
Given here:

Mass density, ρ=ρ01-x2L2 kg m-1

Let us suppose there is an element of length dx at a distance x from left must end.

Mass of this element is dm=ρdx=ρ01-x2L2

The centre of mass of this element is Xcm=∫xdm∫dm

=ρ0∫0lx-x2L2xdx∫0lρ01-x2L2dx

=L22-L44L2L-L33L2=L242L3=3L8

Hence, the value of α=8.

q: A FM Broad cast transmitter, using modulating signal of frequency <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mi>kHz</mi></math> has a deviation ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math>. The Bandwidth required for transmission is :
question: A FM Broad cast transmitter, using modulating signal of frequency <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mi>kHz</mi></math> has a deviation ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math>. The Bandwidth required for transmission is :
solution:
Given

FM broadcast

Modulating frequency =20 kHz=f

Deviation ratio = frequency deviation  modulating frequency =∆ff

⇒ Frequency deviation, Δf=f×10

=20 kHz×10=200 kHz

⇒ Bandwidth =2f+Δf

=220+200 kHz

=440 kHz

q:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : A uniform wire of resistance 80 Ω is cut into four equal parts. These parts are now connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance of the combination will be 5 Ω.

Statement II : Two resistance 2R and 3R are connected in parallel in an electric circuit. The value of thermal energy developed in 3R and 2R will be in the ratio 3:2.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : A uniform wire of resistance 80 Ω is cut into four equal parts. These parts are now connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance of the combination will be 5 Ω.

Statement II : Two resistance 2R and 3R are connected in parallel in an electric circuit. The value of thermal energy developed in 3R and 2R will be in the ratio 3:2.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Both statement I and statement II are correct
Both statement I and statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
solution:
Statement 1- R=80 Ω

When it is cut into 4 equal parts, the resistance of each part will be,

R1=R2=R3=R4=20 Ω

When these resistances are connected in parallel,

Req =204=5 Ω

Hence, the statement I is correct.

Statement 2 -

When the resistances are connected in parallel, the potential difference between them is same. Therefore, the power consumed can be written as,

P=V2R

⇒P1P2=R2R1=23

Hence, statement II is incorrect.

q: An electrical bulb rated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math>, is connected in series with another bulb rated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math>. If the voltage across combination is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>, the power consumed by the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math> bulb will be about _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math>.
question: An electrical bulb rated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math>, is connected in series with another bulb rated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math>. If the voltage across combination is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>, the power consumed by the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math> bulb will be about _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math>.
solution:
Electric power is given by P=V2R, here, V is voltage and R is resistance.

Now, for each bulb, R1=V2P=2202100=484 Ω and  R2=V2P=220260=484106 Ω

Net current through the series combination is I=220484+484×106 A=0.171 A

Hence, the power consumed by the 100 W bulb is P1=I2R1=14.06 W

q: Two streams of photons, possessing energies to five and ten times the work function of metal are incident on the metal surface successively. The ratio of the maximum velocities of the photoelectron emitted, in the two cases respectively, will be
question: Two streams of photons, possessing energies to five and ten times the work function of metal are incident on the metal surface successively. The ratio of the maximum velocities of the photoelectron emitted, in the two cases respectively, will be
solution:
Using Einstein's photoelectric equation,

Maximum kinetic energy, KEmax=E-ϕ, where, E is energy of photon and ϕ is work function.

For two cases, 

12mv12=5ϕ-ϕ=4ϕ and 12mv22=10ϕ-ϕ=9ϕ

Thus, the ratio v1v2=49=23=2:3

question:
For the given circuit the current through battery of 6 V just after closing the switch 'S' will be _____ A.

​​​​​​​
solution:
Just after closing the switch S, inductor behaves like an open circuit, so no current will flow through 2 Ω resistors.

Thus, current through the circuit is I=62+4=1 A (Here 2 Ω and 4 Ω resistors are in series).

solution:
The radiation pressure in case of complete absorption is given by,

P=Ic.

Therefore, the force will be,

F=Ic×A⇒I=FcA

⇒I=7.2×10-9×3×10836×10-4=600 W m-2⇒I=0.06 W cm-2

Time taken by the electron to cross will be, t=1vx=12 s.

ag=Fm=Eem=8me×em=8 m s-2

vy of the electron  when it has crossed the capacitor will be,

Assume there are two identical simple pendulum Clocks-1 is placed on the earth and Clock-2 is placed on a space station located at a height h above the earth surface. Clock-1 and Clock-2 operate at time periods 4 s and 6 s respectively. Then the value of h is -

question:
Assume there are two identical simple pendulum Clocks-1 is placed on the earth and Clock-2 is placed on a space station located at a height h above the earth surface. Clock-1 and Clock-2 operate at time periods 4 s and 6 s respectively. Then the value of h is -
(consider radius of earth RE=6400 km and g on earth 10 m s-2)
solution:
The time period of a simple pendulum is given by, T=2πlg.

Therefore, T∝1g.

The acceleration due to gravity at height h from the surface of the earth is given by, g'=gR+h2

Therefore,

T1T2=g2g1⇒46=RR+h⇒h=R2=3200 km

question: A vessel contains <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of nitrogen gas at a temperature of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. The amount of heat to be transferred to the gas to double the r.m.s. speed of its molecules will be : (Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>32</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
solution:
The RMS speed of a gas at a given temperature is given by, vrms=3RTM.

Therefore, to double the RMS speed, the temperature should be increased to four times the initial temperature. Therefore,

Tf=1200 K, Ti=300 K, and n=1428=12

The volume of the vessel will remain the same therefore, the process is an isochoric process. Therefore,

Q=nCvΔT=12×5R2×900

⇒Q=9360 J

q: A uniform metal chain of mass m and length L passes over a massless and frictionless pulley. It is released from rest with a part of its length l is hanging on one side and rest of its length L-l  is hanging on the other side of the pulley. At a certain point of time, when l=Lx, the acceleration of the chain is g2. The value of x is _____.
question: A uniform metal chain of mass m and length L passes over a massless and frictionless pulley. It is released from rest with a part of its length l is hanging on one side and rest of its length L-l  is hanging on the other side of the pulley. At a certain point of time, when l=Lx, the acceleration of the chain is g2. The value of x is _____.
solution:
According to the information given in the question, the part of the chain that envelopes the pulley is very small. Therefore, the acceleration of the chain can be written as,

a=m2-m1m2+m1g

Since a=g2

g2=m2-m1m2+m1g⇒g2=L-l-lLg⇒l=L4=Lx

⇒x=4

question: A triangular shaped wire carrying 10 A current is placed in a uniform magnetic field of 0.5 T, as shown in figure. The magnetic force on segment CD is (Given BC=CD=BD=5 cm).
solution:
Force due to magnetic field on a current carrying conductor is given by, F→=il→×B→.

The angle between the magnetic field and the direction of the current in the wire CD is 60°. Therefore,

F=ilBsin60°⇒F=10×0.05×0.5×32⇒F=0.216 N
